description.p: ClusterCut is a seamless clipboard synchronization tool that works across Linux, macOS and Windows. It allows you to copy text on one device and paste it on another instantly, keeping your workflow uninterrupted.
Features include:

ul.li: Real-time clipboard synchronization
Secure peer-to-peer connection
Clipboard history
File transfer
Automatic/Manual transfer mode
Cross-platform support
Minimal resource usage

screenshot.caption: Main window, showing cluster membership and join details.
Local clusters are automatically discovered, or you can join a remote one over VPN/Internet.
Clipboard history allows you to paste and re-send previous clipboard contents.
File transfer allows you to send and receive files between devices.
Use an autogenerated or provisioned cluster name/PIN.
Automatic/Manual transfer mode, and auto-download configuration.

description.p: No more notification pile-ups when your network changes.

ul.li: Switching Wi-Fi networks, or connecting or disconnecting a VPN, no longer sends you a "device left" notification for every device in your cluster — and a "device joined" for each one as they come back
Instead, ClusterCut notices your network has changed, quietly keeps looking for your devices in the background, and shows a single "Your network has changed" message

description.p: Your cluster now heals itself — devices come back on their own after VPNs, sleep, and network hiccups.

ul.li: Connect to your cluster over a VPN and your devices appear on their own — no more "Retry Connection" clicks or restarting the app to see everyone
Devices no longer vanish from the list for no reason, or go missing until you restart — if one is briefly unreachable, ClusterCut quietly keeps checking and brings it back as soon as it responds
Waking your computer from sleep no longer empties the device list while the network reconnects; and if you wake up on a different network, the list updates to show what's actually reachable there
A device that joined your cluster while one of your machines was off or away is now recognised automatically when that machine comes back — previously it had to be paired again
A device you removed from the cluster stays removed, and reconnecting to a device no longer briefly shows a duplicate "Manual" entry
Bringing the ClusterCut window to the front now quietly refreshes the device list, so what you see is always current

description.p: Big improvements to joining and leaving clusters, plus shared PINs for provisioned clusters.

ul.li: Provisioned clusters now share a single PIN. When you set up a cluster with a name and PIN, every device that joins picks up that same PIN automatically — you no longer need to set it separately on each device

You can now join a cluster using the PIN of any device already in it, rather than one specific device. Enter any member's PIN and ClusterCut pairs with the right one; if it doesn't match any device that's online, it asks you to try another device's PIN
Leaving a cluster is now reliable — the device you leave on disappears from your other devices right away, and removing a device from your cluster works properly too
When a device leaves and starts a fresh cluster, that new cluster now appears in your nearby list straight away, without having to restart the app

Fixed a problem where a device could sometimes refuse to join because of leftover records from earlier connection attempts — ClusterCut now clears those out automatically so they don't cause trouble later
Devices are now identified in a way that rules out two of them ever being mistaken for the same one

description.p: A clipboard reliability fix and a calmer diagnostics log.

ul.li: Fixed large blocks of text occasionally bouncing back and reappearing on the device you copied from, when you copied something else right afterwards

The Diagnostics event log no longer fills up with routine connection messages at the Minimal level — pairing and any real connection problems still show there, and you can switch to Detailed to see everything

description.p: A Windows crash fix, an instant History list for large items, syncing of large blocks of text, and a quieter GNOME notification.

ul.li: Fixed a crash on Windows that could happen when you copied formatted content — such as text from a web page or document — shortly after copying a large amount of text

Your History list now appears instantly even for very large copied items, like big images or large blocks of text, instead of taking 10–20 seconds. You can also choose how much clipboard content History keeps, under Settings → General
Large blocks of text (over 10 MB) now sync between your devices and still paste as text on the other side. Text above 100 MB isn't sent, and a note appears in your History instead
On GNOME, the "clipboard sync is now active" notification no longer pops up every time you start ClusterCut — it now appears only once, after you install or re-enable the extension

description.p: A reorganised Settings page, more control on locked-down networks, a live diagnostics view, and tighter handling of your private key and PIN.

ul.li: Settings is now organised into a sidebar with categories (General, Cluster, Files, Notifications, Diagnostics) instead of one long scrolling page
Adding a remote device by IP is smarter — if you've paired with it before, it just reconnects; it only asks for a PIN when it's a brand-new device

New options for restrictive or work networks: you can turn off automatic discovery (mDNS) to stay hidden and connect only by IP, and on Windows you can stop ClusterCut from setting up its firewall rule at startup

Your cluster's name now stays in sync across all your devices — rename it on one and the others update too, even ones that were offline at the time. Switching back to an auto-generated name asks for confirmation first, since it renames the cluster everywhere

New diagnostics event log under Settings → Diagnostics: watch pairing and connection activity as it happens, with selectable detail levels and a one-click copy for troubleshooting. It lives only in the app and is never written to a file

Security: your device's private key and pairing PIN are now saved with locked-down, owner-only permissions; in auto-generated mode the PIN is kept in memory only and never written to disk; and PINs are never written to the log file

description.p: Clipboard and pairing fixes, plus smoother formatted-text handling on GNOME.

ul.li: Fixed a banner that wrongly told you to re-pair a device when you were connected through a VPN
Pairing with the wrong PIN now shows a clear "the PIN may be incorrect" message instead of a confusing "session expired" error, and an accidental space in the PIN no longer silently breaks pairing
Copying formatted text to a Windows PC no longer clears the clipboard on the computer you copied from
Pasting code copied from PyCharm into Word on Windows now keeps the whole snippet instead of just the last few characters
A stray space, tab, or blank line on your clipboard is no longer pushed to your other devices

On GNOME, receiving formatted text now puts the plain text on your clipboard straight away and offers a one-click "Switch to Rich" button in a notification — so pasting into apps like Text Editor, OnlyOffice, or your browser just works

description.p: Stronger pairing security, an on-demand pause button for pairing, and clearer feedback when a peer is on an older version.

ul.li: New pairing-pause button in the toolbar — click it to stop accepting new pairing requests when you don't want anyone joining your cluster. The same button also lights up red when pairing has been automatically paused after too many failed attempts in a row

Pairing security has been further strengthened — a device trying to join your cluster must now prove it knows the right PIN before your device sends any encrypted reply, so someone trying random PINs can no longer gather data from your device to crack them later

Trying to pair with a device on an older version of ClusterCut now shows the "please upgrade" message right away instead of waiting for the pairing attempt to time out. The amber warning indicator next to a peer's name also now correctly fires for any peer running an older release
Fixed: the Settings tab was quietly saving the same settings once per second in the background even when nothing had changed
Pairing new devices requires both sides to be on 0.3.3 or later — already-paired devices continue to work as before, even if some are still on older versions

description.p: Easier pairing over VPN / restrictive networks, a clearer PIN, and a one-click history wipe.

ul.li: "Add Remote Peer" now actually works when local discovery is blocked (e.g. you're connecting over a VPN, or your firewall is filtering multicast). Type the peer's IP and you'll be asked for its PIN — same as pairing a device you found nearby

Your own PIN is now hidden behind dots on the main screen by default, with a new eye button next to the copy button to reveal it. The copy button still works whether it's revealed or not — handy if you're showing your screen to someone
The PIN label is now "My Cluster PIN" and Settings explains that each device keeps its own PIN; pairing never shares it
New "Clear" button on the Clipboard History view wipes every entry from this device in one go, with a confirmation. Other devices in the cluster keep their own history
Fixed: after joining a cluster you no longer briefly appear in the peer list as a peer of yourself

description.p: Stronger pairing security and clearer handling of failed pairing attempts.

ul.li: Pairing security has been strengthened — someone else on your network can no longer interfere with the pairing handshake, even if they observe the messages going back and forth
After several failed pairing attempts in a row, your device pauses incoming pairing and shows a notification so you can re-enable it when you're ready
New "Verbose pairing logs" option under Settings → Diagnostics, useful if you ever need to troubleshoot why a pairing isn't completing
All your devices need to be on 0.3.1 or later — devices running 0.3.0 cannot talk to this release

description.p: Image and rich-text clipboard sync, plus stronger security.

ul.li: Image clipboard sync — copy a picture (from a browser, a screenshot tool, etc.) and it's ready to paste on your other devices, into any app
Rich-text clipboard sync — formatting from Word, Pages, your browser, Apple Mail and similar apps now comes across to your other devices intact
Pairing and clipboard traffic have been redesigned for stronger security
If a device in your cluster is running a version too old to keep up, ClusterCut now flags which one and prompts you to upgrade
Clipboard history now scrolls properly when the list gets long

Windows reliability — images paste cleanly into apps like Paint and Word, notifications about new devices and incoming files no longer get dropped, and the firewall rule now covers everything ClusterCut needs
macOS Pages now preserves formatting when pasting rich text received from another device
All your devices need to be on 0.3.0 or later — older versions cannot talk to this release

description.p: Faster file transfers and tighter privacy for LAN-only setups.

ul.li: Optional file transfer compression — speeds up sending large text, code, or log files. Off by default; turn it on under Settings → File Transfer once all your devices are on 0.2.3 or later, since older versions can't read compressed transfers
ClusterCut no longer fetches fonts from the internet at startup, keeping the app fully self-contained on your local network

description.p: Better GNOME Wayland support and clearer status reporting.

ul.li: File copies now sync on GNOME Wayland — copy a file in Nautilus, paste it on another peer
Clipboard sync starts automatically as soon as the ClusterCut GNOME extension is enabled; no app restart needed
Notifications now tell you when clipboard sync can't start (missing or outdated extension) or has been paused
Current sync mode is shown in GNOME's Background Apps list, matching the Quick Settings toggle
Requires ClusterCut GNOME extension v3.0 or newer

description.p: Fewer spurious notifications and better handling of suspend/resume and Wi-Fi changes.

ul.li: Far fewer false "Device Joined" / "Device Left" notifications after sleep/wake or reconnecting to Wi-Fi
Peers on a previous network are now cleaned up silently when the local IP changes
Windows: the firewall rule check avoids the repeated UAC prompt on every launch

description.p: Clipboard sync now works natively on Wayland. No more X11 workarounds.

ul.li: Full Wayland support for KDE Plasma, Sway, and Hyprland
GNOME Wayland support via the ClusterCut extension (install from GNOME Extensions)
Updated GNOME extension to v2.0 with clipboard bridging
Flatpak runtime updated to GNOME 50
Flatpak: added X11 socket for clipboard support on Wayland (via XWayland)
Tray icon now visible on KDE/Plasma dark panels (symbolic icon override is limited to GNOME-like desktops)

description.p: Updated Flatpak permission requests.

ul.li: Fixed tray icon appearance on non-Flatpak Linux installs
Improved appearance of Flathub app page
Notifications now use the Notification portal for better Flatpak integration
Fixed autostart command being double-wrapped by the Background portal
Fixed window being invisible when starting minimized and then showing from tray
License changed to GPL-3.0-or-later

description.p: Autostart now uses the Background portal for better integration with Flatpak and GNOME.
Tray icon now uses a GNOME symbolic style with badge support for a more native look.
Flatpak packaging improvements.
Device join and leave notifications are now more reliable.
